caption: The video appears to be an educational lecture on cryogenic engineering, specifically focusing on the performance of a Linde-Hampson system. The slide displayed is titled "Performance of L-H System" and contains a table comparing various fluids—nitrogen (N₂), argon (Ar), air, and oxygen (O₂)—based on their boiling points, specific heat ratios (γ), work per unit mass (W/m), cooling power per unit mass (Wc/m), and figure of merit (FOM). The data is presented for conditions where pressures range from 1 bar to 200 bar at a temperature of 300K. The instructor, Prof. M D Atrey from the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Bombay, likely discusses how different fluids perform under these specific conditions, highlighting the efficiency and effectiveness of each fluid in cryogenic applications. The cursor movement suggests that the presenter is pointing out or explaining specific details within the table, possibly emphasizing the significance of the FOM values or other critical parameters for each fluid.
